page: Amazon Web Services i7i.metal-24xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C processors built on the x86_64 architecture. It delivers 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.4 GHz and a 320 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, it bundles 22500 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity, paired with a baseline network bandwidth of 56 Gbps. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results show top-tier performance in several areas. The server achieves top 10% placement in Passmark CPU scores, Passmark database operations, and Redis SET operations, which reached 122.39 million operations per second. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput also rank in the top 10% tier. Memory bandwidth tests demonstrate strong top 25% performance, reaching 289.55 GB/sec for large-block read-only operations. In LLM inference tests using a medium 7B model, prompt processing speeds fall into the top 10% tier, while text generation speeds remain in the top 25% tier.

As a bare-metal instance, the i7i.metal-24xl offers high resource density and direct hardware access, eliminating virtualization overhead. The combination of massive local NVMe SSD storage and high memory bandwidth provides qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive workloads, though the lack of GPU acceleration limits its utility for heavy deep learning training. This server is optimized for large-scale database deployments, high-throughput Redis caching, data warehousing, and high-concurrency web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services i7i.metal-24xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server powered by dedicated Intel Xeon 8559C processors on the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 vCPUs, 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory, and 22500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, alongside a 56 Gbps baseline network speed. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving, alongside strong memory bandwidth. Lacking GPU hardware, the server relies on advanced CPU instruction sets like AVX-512 and AMX for acceleration. This bare-metal configuration is engineered for high-throughput database systems, large-scale caching layers, and intensive data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services i7i.metal-24xl is a storage-optimized bare-metal server with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 768 GB DDR5 RAM, and 22,500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-throughput database workloads.

tagline: A bare-metal, storage-optimized server combining 22.5 TB of local NVMe SSDs with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by Intel Xeon 8559C processors
768.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
22500 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed I/O operations
Top-tier database performance, reaching 122.39 million Redis SET operations per second
56 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfers
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and data-intensive workloads
